1.11a: Contact point for standard documents: Issuing office
1.11b: Contact point if standard documents are inappropriate: Issuing office
1.7: Lending is not permitted where the borrower and mortgagor are not the same party.
1.14: The firm may not act if the person dealing with the transaction or their immediate family is the seller.
1.15: The firm may not act if the person dealing with the transaction or their immediate family is the borrower.
3.1.3: Notification of the seller's solicitor details is not required.
The firm must ensure the seller's solicitor is registered with a regulatory body and holds a current practicing certificate.
3.1.4: Lender must be notified if the seller is unrepresented or if identity checks on the seller's representative are unsatisfactory.
3.2.3: Notification of the seller's solicitor details is not required.
The firm must verify the seller's legal representative is registered and maintains current practicing certificates.
4.1: The lender provides a valuation report but does not supply it to the solicitor.
4.5b: Certificate of title must be sent to the issuing office as per 1.11a.
5.1.1: Contact issuing office if the seller has owned the property for less than 6 months.
Sub-sales and back-to-back transactions are not acceptable.
Assignable contracts and irrevocable powers of attorney in favor of intervening sellers are not acceptable.
Transactions where the seller acquires title and immediately grants a lease to the borrower are considered sub-sales and are prohibited.
5.2.1: Contact issuing office if the seller is not the registered owner.
Sub-sales, back-to-back transactions, and assigned contracts for sale are prohibited.
Parties must comply with the Economic Crime (Transparency and Enforcement) Act 2022.
5.4.4: Lender does not require environmental reports but must be advised of contamination entries in local authority searches.
Japanese Knotweed Category C (RICS) is acceptable if a management/treatment plan by a Qualified Person (PCA/INNSA) is in place prior to completion.
Japanese Knotweed Category C requires a 10-year insurance-backed guarantee.
Japanese Knotweed Category D (outside boundary) is acceptable without a treatment plan.
Mundic property is acceptable only if RICS Classes A, A/B, A1, A2, or A3.
Mundic reports must be dated after 1 January 1997 or be assigned to the borrower/mortgagee.
Mundic Categories B and C are not acceptable.
5.4.5: Personal searches are acceptable if the firm subscribes to the Search Code (PCCB regulated) and an unqualified Certificate of Title is provided.
5.4.6: Search insurance is accepted for re-mortgages only.
For Limited Companies purchasing from a director, search insurance is acceptable if the solicitor confirms no material risk.
5.5.3b: Original planning permissions are not required by the lender but must be retained on the solicitor's file.
5.5.3c: Building regulation consents must be retained on the solicitor's file, not sent to the lender.
5.5.3d: Certificates of lawful use or development must be retained on the solicitor's file, not sent to the lender.
5.7.1a: Flying freeholds are acceptable up to 10% of the total property area.
Valuer must confirm the exact percentage.
5.7.1b: Freehold flats are not acceptable security.
5.7.1c: Flying freeholds do not need to be reported if they are under 10% and meet Part 1 requirements.
5.8.1: Security comprising up to four flats where the borrower owns the freehold is acceptable if the freehold is charged to the Bank.
If the freeholder is an entity connected to the borrower, an additional Charge of the freehold is required.
Stock transfer forms for company-owned freeholds must be held in escrow.
5.8.5: Arrangements involving two leasehold flats where owners cross-own freeholds are accepted.
The borrower's leasehold interest and their freehold interest in the other flat must both be charged.
5.13.1: The deposit must be from the borrower's own resources.
Director loans are acceptable for Limited Company BTL if non-interest bearing, the price is at market value, and it is documented in company accounts.
Gifted family deposits < £20,000 require a deed of gift or letter dated within 3 months of completion.
Gifted family deposits >= £20,000 are typically handled before the mortgage offer.
Deposit givers must be resident in the UK or EEA and funds must originate from the UK or EEA.
Clear bankruptcy searches are required for all parties contributing to the purchase price.
Deed of gift indemnity insurance must be implemented if applicable.
5.14.1: Minimum unexpired lease term must be at least 50 years at the end of the mortgage term.
5.14.9: Report if ground rent exceeds 0.1% of the purchase price or valuation.
Report if ground rent increases by more than 25% upon review.
Report if ground rent is reviewed more frequently than every 25 years.
5.14.11: Indemnity insurance for unsatisfactory lease terms is acceptable if it covers the specific defect and meets Part 1 requirements.
5.14.12: Solicitor must ensure ground rent and service charges are paid to date at completion; receipts are not required by lender.
5.14.13: Notice of assignment must be served regardless of lease provisions, but evidence is not sent to lender.
5.14.15a: Absentee/insolvent landlords are acceptable only if there are 6 or fewer flats and indemnity insurance is in place.
5.14.15b: Indemnity insurance for absent/insolvent landlords is accepted subject to a maximum of 6 flats in the building.
5.14.17b: Safety instructions apply to buildings ≥ 11m height or ≥ 5 storeys.
Report if the security property requires remediation or if the owner is a non-qualifying leaseholder.
Do not complete until written instructions are received regarding remediation costs.
5.15.2a: Estate Rent Charges are acceptable if Section 121 Law of Property Act is excluded.
Estate Rent Charges are acceptable if there is a 28-day notice period for the mortgagee prior to enforcement.
Estate Rent Charges are acceptable if the owner is a management company comprising resident shareholders.
5.15.2b: Management company share certificates should not be sent to the lender.
5.15.2c: Signed blank stock transfer forms must be sent to the lender.
5.15.2d: Management company articles must not restrict the transfer of ownership.
5.17.5a: A certified copy of the Power of Attorney must be sent after completion.
5.17.5b: A certified copy of the statutory declaration of non-revocation must be sent after completion.
5.20.1: Solar PV leases not meeting UK Finance minimum requirements must be reported.
6.3.1: Distressed sales and sale-and-rent-back arrangements are not acceptable.
6.4.4: All incentives must be reported including nature and amount.
Report if an equity share deposit source is not a family gift or own resources.
6.5.1: Vacant possession is required unless the mortgage offer explicitly permits letting.
6.6.3: Only Assured Shorthold Tenancy (AST) agreements are acceptable.
6.6.4: HMO properties must have valid planning permission, certificate of lawful use, or permitted development rights (PDR).
If Article 4 direction applies, solicitor must obtain historical ASTs or HMO licenses and a statutory declaration to prove continuous use.
Title must contain no restrictions on HMO use unless indemnity insurance is obtained.
6.7.1: Acceptable warranties: NHBC, Advantage, HomeProof, Ark Residential, BOPAS, Build Assure, Build Zone, Building Life Plan, CADIS, Checkmate, Global Home, ICW, LABC, One Guarantee, Premier, Protek, Q Policy, Zurich Municipal, ABC+ (if appointed at commencement).
Retrospective new build warranties and Professional Consultants Certificates (PCC) are not acceptable.
Disclosure of Incentives Form (DIF) required for all new builds.
6.7.4: Professional Consultant's Certificate (PCC) is accepted with proof of PI insurance; must not be retrospective.
6.7.6: Professional consultant's certificate should be retained on file, not sent.
6.9.1: Absent easements do not need reporting if indemnity insurance is in place.
6.10.2: Retentions are released directly to the borrowers.
7.3: Consent to mortgage required from all occupants aged 17 or over, except for BTL arrangements.
8.1: Firms may act for third parties if there is no conflict of interest and independent legal advice is provided.
9.1: Indemnity insurance policies must be sent to the lender upon or after completion.
9.2: Indemnity limit must be at least 110% of the mortgage valuation.
10.2a: Advance is paid electronically via CHAPS.
10.2b: Minimum of 5 working days notice is required for the release of funds.
10.3: Deductions from advance: CHAPS fee, unpaid admin fees, unpaid product fees.
10.7: Delayed completion funds must be returned within 5 working days using the same method they were received.
10.10: Advance may be held for a maximum of 5 working days.
10.11: Interest is charged from the date of release; interest is waived if funds are returned within 5 working days.
14.2.1: Pre-completion: Send guarantees/personal guarantees to completions@krbs.com.

Post-completion (within 28 days): Send copy of legal charge, registration evidence (Companies House/Land Registry), NHBC docs, notices of assignment, landlord undertakings, indemnity insurance, and occupier consents to generaldespatcher@krbs.com.
14.2.2: Only the executed stock transfer form for section 5.8.1 must be sent post-completion.
16.3.7b: Attestation clause: 'Signed on behalf of OneSavings Bank plc Acting by its *Director / *Attorney in the presence of: [Witness Details]'.
16.3.4: Transfer of equity documents must be sent to the lender.
16.5.3a: Deeds of variation to: PO BOX 600, Chatham, ME4 9DW.
17.1.1: Redemption statements: Lending Department, PO BOX 600, Chatham, ME4 9DW.
17.2.1a: Discharge and repayment remittance: Lending Department, PO BOX 600, Chatham, ME4 9DW.
17.2.1b: Discharge is handled directly with the Land Registry.
